Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 11:32 ET
Massive Tech Flow Contradicts Bearish Regime as Dealers Amplify Every Move
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. MSFT and AMZN saw enormous call block activity despite oversold technicals and negative $18.2B dealer gamma that amplifies all moves.
  • 2. Index puts dominate (1.38 P/C) while single-name flow stays balanced (0.94 P/C) — classic institutional dispersion hedge setup.
  • 3. Core PCE in hours with negative gamma environment creates explosive potential for any surprise.
NARRATIVE — negative gamma amplified

Tech giants are attracting massive bullish flow despite sitting in oversold territory and operating under the most amplified volatility regime in months. MSFT drew 70,000 calls across May strikes despite death cross technals and RSI at 26, while AMZN saw 20,000 calls despite similar bearish technicals — this contradicts the VIX 29.2 fear environment and suggests institutional conviction buying into weakness. The $18.2B negative dealer gamma means every directional move gets amplified, making these size bets particularly bold. The structural story emerges in the P/C divergence: index puts heavily outweigh calls (1.38 ratio) while single-name flow remains balanced (0.94 ratio). This represents the textbook institutional dispersion hedge — portfolio protection via index puts while maintaining individual stock upside through calls. The 0.29 implied correlation confirms this stock-specific regime, creating clear dispersion opportunities for those long single-name vol and short index vol. Core PCE hits at 8:30am with negative gamma amplifying any surprise by 3-4x normal magnitude. The vol surface shows extreme divergences — IBIT, XLK showing rich vol but flat skew while cybersecurity names like CRWD trade 235% rich to sector. With NFP just 6 days out and dealers forced to hedge aggressively, any PCE deviation from consensus becomes immediately dangerous.

Layer 1: VIX 29.2, sentiment -57Layer 2: SPY GEX -$18.2BLayer 3: Tech call blocks vs index putsLayer 9: Core PCE today
MSFTBULLISH
Oversold Tech Conviction
Massive 70,000 call blocks across May 405C and 445C strikes despite death cross technicals and RSI at oversold 26. Size suggests institutional conviction buying into technical weakness ahead of PCE.

Hit vol divergence flag with rich vol but heavy call flow via 20,000 June call blocks. Creates tension between expensive premium and bullish positioning, suggesting vol sellers vs directional buyers.
